title: Hepatitis B — Chronic HBV with Cirrhosis

patient_data.chief_complaint: Progressive abdominal distension, jaundice, fatigue for 3 months
patient_data.history: Born in endemic region (Southeast Asia). No childhood hepatitis B vaccination. History of intravenous drug use in 20s. Known HBsAg-positive for 10 years, never treated. No prior liver imaging.

expected_output.primary_diagnosis: Chronic hepatitis B with decompensated cirrhosis
should_detect: Chronic hepatitis B — WHO global hepatitis elimination target
Decompensated cirrhosis (Child-Pugh C likely): jaundice, ascites, coagulopathy, low albumin
Hepatic encephalopathy suspected — confusion + elevated ammonia
Elevated AFP (245) — hepatocellular carcinoma screening urgently needed
High HBV viral load needs antiviral therapy — tenofovir or entecavir
Nucleos(t)ide analogue therapy is WHO-recommended first-line
Liver transplant evaluation may be needed
